title: System for filtering and in situ monitoring of working fluids
abstract: A working fluid monitoring system includes a filter member (104) having an inlet (114), an outlet (118), and a filter media (116) disposed between the inlet and the outlet. The filter member (104) is configured to permit fluid communication of the working fluid from the inlet, through the filter media, and out the outlet of the filter member. A sensor (80) is in operable communication with the working fluid within the filter member and is configured to monitor in situ a parameter of the working fluid.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A working fluid monitoring system for monitoring a working fluid of working fluid system of a piece of equipment, the working fluid monitoring system comprising: a filter member having an inlet, an outlet, and a filter media disposed between the inlet and the outlet, the filter member being configured for fluid communication of the working fluid of the working fluid system from the inlet, through the filter media, and out the outlet; and a sensor in operable communication with the working fluid within the filter member, the sensor being configured to monitor in situ a parameter of the working fluid. 2. The working fluid monitoring system of Claim 1 wherein the sensor is configured to monitor in situ a temperature or pressure parameter of the working fluid. 3. The working fluid monitoring system of any one of Claims 1 or 2 wherein the sensor is configured to monitor in situ a parameter of the working fluid chosen from the group consisting of suspended soot, dielectric, conductivity, capacitance, total acid number, total base number, pH, oxidation, viscosity, flow rate, water, coolant, density, oil viscosity grade, VI, SSI (viscosity modifier shear stability), ferrous iron, fuel dilution, RGB color, florescence, phosphorescence, UV, infrared, X-ray, XRF, RF, refractive index, varnish, deposits, iron, lead, copper, aluminum, tin, vanadium, zinc, phosphorus, boron, calcium, magnesium, sulfur, particle count, and air concentration. 4. The working fluid monitoring system of any one of Claims 1 to 3 wherein the sensor is configured to monitor in situ a parameter of the working fluid system. 5. An oil filter adapter assembly for monitoring oil parameters of a lubrication system of an engine, the engine having an oil filter stud and an associated oil filter, the oil filter adapter assembly comprising: an adapter having a body having a proximal face and a distal face, the proximal face sealingly engageable with the engine and the distal face sealingly engageable with the oil filter, the adapter further having a central bore and an annular passageway, the annular passageway being configured to fluidly couple the lubrication system of the engine and the oil filter and receive oil therein, a first pressure port fluidly extending from an exterior of the adapter to the annular passageway, the first pressure port configured to be operably coupled to a sensor; and a fastener having a body defining a retaining feature, the fastener having a proximal end and a distal end, the proximal end having a threaded portion configured to threadedly engage the oil filter stud of the engine such that the retaining feature captures the adapter and retains the adapter in sealing engagement with the engine, the distal end having a threaded portion configured to threadedly engage the oil filter, the fastener further having a central oil passageway being 

 configured to fiuidly couple the lubrication system of the engine and the oil filter and receive oil therein. 6. The oil filter adapter assembly according to Claim 5 further comprising: a second pressure port fluidly extending from an exterior of the fastener to the central oil passageway, the second pressure port configured to be operably coupled to a sensor via a communication port in fluid communication with the second pressure port. 7. The oil filter adapter assembly according to any one of Claims 5 or 6, wherein the communication port extends from the exterior of the adapter to the second pressure port. 8. The oil filter adapter assembly according to any one of Claims 5 to 7, wherein the fastener comprises a fluidic channel extending circumferentially about the fastener, the fluidic channel providing fluid communication between the second pressure port and the communication port. 9. The oil filter adapter assembly according to Claims 5 to 8, wherein the fastener comprises a pair of sealing members extending circumferentially about the fastener on opposing sides of the fluidic channel. 10. The oil filter adapter assembly according to any one of Claims 5 to 9 wherein the annular passageway comprises a plurality of annular passageways configured to fiuidly couple the lubrication system of the engine and the oil filter and receive oil therein. 11. The oil filter adapter assembly according to any one of Claims 5 to 10 wherein the plurality of annular passageways are disposed within an annulus area between the central bore and an outer portion of the body thereby defining radially disposed spokes between the plurality of annular passageways. 12. The oil filter adapter assembly according to any one of Claims 5 to 11 further comprising: a second pressure port fluidly extending from an exterior of the fastener to the central oil passageway, the second pressure port configured to be operably coupled to a sensor via a communication port in fluid communication with the second pressure port, the communication port extending from the exterior of the adapter through at least one of the radially disposed spokes to the second pressure port. 13. The oil filter adapter assembly according to any one of Claims 5 to 12, further comprising a sealing member extending from the proximal face of the body configured to be engaged with the engine. 14. The oil filter adapter assembly according to any one of Claims 5 to 13, further comprising the sensor, the sensor being configured to sense a parameter of the oil. 15. An oil filter adapter assembly for monitoring oil parameters of a lubrication system of an engine, the engine having an oil filter stud and an associated oil filter, the oil filter adapter assembly comprising: an adapter having a body having a proximal face and a distal face, the proximal face sealingly engageable with the engine and the distal face sealingly engageable with the oil filter, the adapter further having a central bore and an annular passageway, the annular passageway being configured to fluidly couple the lubrication system of the engine and the oil filter and receive oil therein; and a fastener having a body defining a retaining feature, the fastener having a proximal end and a distal end, the proximal end having a threaded portion configured to threadedly engage the oil filter stud of the engine such that the retaining feature captures the adapter and retains the adapter in sealing engagement with the engine, the distal end having a threaded portion configured to threadedly engage the oil filter, the fastener further having a central oil passageway being configured to fluidly couple the lubrication system of the engine and the oil filter and receive oil therein, a first pressure port fluidly extending from an exterior of the fastener to the central oil passageway, the first pressure port configured to be operably coupled to a sensor via a communication port in fluid communication with the first pressure port. 16. The oil filter adapter assembly according to Claim 15, wherein the communication port extends from the exterior of the adapter to the first pressure port. 17. The oil filter adapter assembly according to any one of Claims 15 or 16, wherein the fastener comprises a fluidic channel extending circumferentially about the fastener, the fluidic channel providing fluid communication between the second pressure port and the communication port. 18. The oil filter adapter assembly according to any one of Claims 15 to 17, wherein the fastener comprises a pair of sealing members extending circumferentially about the fastener on opposing sides of the fluidic channel. 19. The oil filter adapter assembly according to any one of Claims 15 to 18 further comprising: a second pressure port fluidly extending from an exterior of the adapter to the annular passageway, the second pressure port configured to be operably coupled to a sensor. 20. The oil filter adapter assembly according to any one of Claims 15 to 19 wherein the annular passageway comprises a plurality of annular passageways configured to fluidly couple the lubrication system of the engine and the oil filter and receive oil therein. 21. The oil filter adapter assembly according to any one of Claims 15 to 20 wherein the plurality of annular passageways are disposed within an annulus area between the central bore and an outer 

 portion of the body thereby defining radially disposed spokes between the plurality of annular passageways. 22. The oil filter adapter assembly according to any one of Claims 15 to 21 further comprising: a second pressure port fluidly extending from an exterior of the fastener to the central oil passageway, the second pressure port configured to be operably coupled to a sensor via a communication port in fluid communication with the second pressure port, the communication port extending from the exterior of the adapter through at least one of the radially disposed spokes to the second pressure port. 23. The oil filter adapter assembly according to any one of Claims 15 to 22, further comprising a sealing member extending from the proximal face of the body configured to be engaged with the engine. 24. The oil filter adapter assembly according to any one of Claims 15 to 23, further comprising the sensor, the sensor being configured to sense a parameter of the oil.
